nodes.label: Sentinel Agent
nodes.rationale: Archetype: sentinel - initializes project, spawns orchestrator, monitors progress. Working directory: /home/bank/score-board/.agents/. No technical decisions - relay only. Victory Audit is MANDATORY before reporting completion.

nodes.label: ScoreBoard Original User Request
nodes.rationale: Mobile-first Next.js score-tracking app for tabletop/card games. Core requirements: UI implementation matching design specs, Setup Screen with up to 10 players, In-Game Screen with leader indicator and stopwatch, state persistence, E2E verification suite. Integrity mode: benchmark.

nodes.label: Forensic Auditor Agent
nodes.rationale: Conducts full integrity forensic review of milestone implementations. Verifies no hardcoded test results, no mock/dummy implementations, authentic coding practices. Audit-only role - does NOT modify implementation code. Trust NOTHING - verify everything independently.

nodes.label: Milestone 1 Clean Audit Verdict
nodes.rationale: First audit of Milestone 1 (Setup Screen and global state hook) resulted in CLEAN verdict. No hardcoded outputs, genuine state transitions, no pre-populated artifacts, successful build, dependency audit passed. E2E test failed due to locator ambiguity, not integrity issue.

nodes.label: PlayerDialog Color Validation Bug
nodes.rationale: Functional bug: When editing a player and keeping their original color, the duplicate color check incorrectly blocks saving. The dialog's usedColors list contains their own color, causing false positive duplicate error. Blast radius: users cannot edit player names without changing color.

nodes.label: Playwright Locator Ambiguity Issue
nodes.rationale: E2E test failure: locator('text=Add Player') resolves to 3 elements (warning message, ADD PLAYER button, dialog header). Playwright strict mode violation causes test suite failure. Mitigation: use specific role-based locator like page.getByRole('heading', { name: 'Add Player' }).

nodes.label: Challenger Agent
nodes.rationale: Archetype: Challenger / critic / specialist. Empirically verifies correctness and robustness of codebase. Checks for edge cases, empty values, inputs exceeding 40 chars, potential state bugs. Validates layout and accessibility targets. Review-only - does NOT modify implementation code.

nodes.label: Color Lock Bug Confirmation
nodes.rationale: Confirmed bug in PlayerDialog.tsx: duplicate color check does not exclude player's own color when editing. usedColors prop includes all player colors including the one being edited. Verification script confirmed PlayerDialog blocks saving when color unchanged.

nodes.label: Palette Size Limit Bug
nodes.rationale: 9th player cap bug: PALETTE in useGameState.ts has only 8 colors. With strict unique color enforcement, adding 9th player always fails validation. Application limit of 10 players is unreachable due to palette size constraint.

nodes.label: React Asynchronous State Update Bug
nodes.rationale: Critical bug in useGameState.ts: addSetupPlayer, updateSetupPlayer, and addPlayerInGame use local success variable modified inside React state updater. Functions return success synchronously before React schedules state updates, causing false positives even when constraint validation fails.

nodes.label: Accessibility Input Label Gap
nodes.rationale: Accessibility issue: Input labels and fields in SetupScreen.tsx and PlayerDialog.tsx lack programmatic linking. Missing htmlFor on labels and id attributes on inputs for title and player name fields. Fails screen reader accessibility and form interaction requirements.

nodes.label: Milestone 1 Fixed Clean Verdict
nodes.rationale: After fixes, forensic audit resulted in CLEAN verdict. All verification tests pass: Playwright E2E (4 tests passed), unit/verification scripts confirm color edit bug fixed, palette expanded to 12 colors, synchronous validation working. No hardcoded values or facade implementations.

nodes.label: Palette Expansion to 12 Colors
nodes.rationale: Fix for 9th player bug: PALETTE expanded from 8 to 12 unique colors in useGameState.ts. Prevents setup blocks when adding 9th+ players. Enables full 10-player capacity with color uniqueness constraint intact.

nodes.label: Color Duplicate Check Fix
nodes.rationale: Fix for color lock bug: PlayerDialog.tsx updated to exclude player's own color from duplicate check. Uses condition: colorLower !== initialColor.toLowerCase(). Similar fix in useGameState.ts line 139 checks p.id !== id. Allows editing player names without changing colors.

nodes.label: Accessibility Label-Input Wiring Fix
nodes.rationale: Fix for accessibility gap: SetupScreen.tsx and PlayerDialog.tsx updated with proper label-input wiring. Added htmlFor attribute on labels and matching id attributes on inputs. Correct HTML semantics for screen reader accessibility and form interaction.

nodes.label: Verification Test Suite
nodes.rationale: Custom stress-testing suite created: verify-all.js tests 10-player sequential addition, 11th player blocking, deletions, duplicate color validation, name edits without color change, color changes. All tests PASS, confirming fixes work correctly.

nodes.label: Explorer Agent
nodes.rationale: Archetype: Explorer. Read-only investigator and analyzer. Investigates codebase, designs implementations, documents findings. Does NOT implement. Code-only network mode (no external web access). Creates handoff reports with findings and design proposals.

nodes.label: Milestone 2 In-Game Screen Investigation
nodes.rationale: Explorer investigation for Milestone 2 (In-Game Screen Base & Leader Calculation). Key findings: useGameState hook already has essential state structures and actions. Need to create InGameScreen component. E2E tests specify exact aria-labels and roles. Leader label is 'LEADER' for highest score, 'PLAYER N' for others. Score container needs aria-live attribute.

nodes.label: In-Game Screen Component Design
nodes.rationale: Separate presentational component InGameScreen.tsx to handle all in-game UI rendering including header with editable title, player list with leader calculation, stopwatch banner, and bulk action bar

nodes.label: Dynamic Leader Calculation Algorithm
nodes.rationale: Linear scan with strict inequality (>) ensures first player retains leader badge in ties. Computed dynamically on each render rather than storing state to avoid sync issues.

nodes.label: Accessibility Implementation Strategy
nodes.rationale: ARIA labels and live regions for screen readers, dynamic aria-label generation using player names for increment/decrement buttons, aria-live=polite on score displays

nodes.label: Player Color Consistency Design Pattern
nodes.rationale: Player accent color must propagate to four UI surfaces: left-border (setup), avatar background, score text (in-game), and + button. Never hardcode single color for all players.

nodes.label: Global State Hook Architecture
nodes.rationale: React Context Provider for global state management to avoid prop-drilling. Includes screen routing, active game session, setup configuration, and stopwatch interval.

nodes.label: SSR Hydration Safety Pattern
nodes.rationale: Defer localStorage reads to useEffect to prevent Next.js hydration mismatches. Initialize with default states and track hydration via isHydrated boolean.

nodes.label: Non-Blocking Stopwatch Design
nodes.rationale: Functional state update callback (prev => ...) ensures stopwatch only depends on isRunning state, preventing timer resets from unrelated re-renders.

nodes.label: Color Uniqueness Validation System
nodes.rationale: 12-color palette supports 10 players while preventing duplicate colors. Colors selected by other players are disabled in picker during add/edit operations.

nodes.label: E2E Test Requirements for In-Game Screen
nodes.norm_label: e2e test requirements for in-game screen

nodes.label: Duplicate Color Validation Bug
nodes.rationale: Critical bug found in PlayerDialog where editing a player's name while keeping their color triggered duplicate color validation error due to usedColors containing the player's own color

nodes.label: Color Palette Exhaustion Bug
nodes.rationale: Major bug where only 8 colors existed in PALETTE but requirements specified support for 10 players, making it impossible to add more than 8 players due to unique color constraint

nodes.label: Bug Fix Verification Process
nodes.rationale: Systematic verification of four previously reported bugs: Edit Player lockout, Palette size expansion to 12 colors, Synchronous returns for validation functions, and Accessibility label associations

nodes.label: SSR Hydration Mismatch Protection
nodes.rationale: Next.js SSR safety mechanism using isInitialized state to prevent hydration mismatches between server and client renders

nodes.label: Accessibility Label Implementation
nodes.rationale: Proper htmlFor/id associations between label and input elements for screen reader compatibility in game title and player name inputs

nodes.label: Synchronous Validation Pattern
nodes.rationale: Validation functions perform checks directly on current render scope state variables and return boolean values synchronously rather than relying on state setter updates

nodes.label: Four-Tier E2E Testing Strategy
nodes.rationale: Comprehensive E2E testing methodology with Tier 1 (40 happy-path tests), Tier 2 (40 boundary/corner tests), Tier 3 (8 cross-feature interaction tests), Tier 4 (5 real-world scenario tests)

nodes.label: Opaque-Box Testing Approach
nodes.rationale: Requirement-driven E2E testing methodology that treats the application as a black box, testing only through user interface without dependency on implementation details

nodes.label: Feature Inventory Management
nodes.rationale: Systematic cataloging of 8 core features for comprehensive test coverage: Game Setup, Add Player, Edit/Delete Players, Stopwatch, Individual Actions, Leader Tracking, Bulk Actions, End Game Flow

nodes.label: Test Coverage Thresholds
nodes.rationale: Minimum test count requirements per tier: Tier 1 (≥5 per feature = 40 total), Tier 2 (≥5 per feature = 40 total), Tier 3 (pairwise coverage = 8 total), Tier 4 (≥5 scenarios = 5 total)

nodes.label: Playwright Test Implementation
nodes.rationale: Implementation of 93 custom E2E tests using Playwright framework with automatic Next.js dev server startup and headless Chromium execution

nodes.label: E2E Feature Coverage Testing
nodes.rationale: Happy-path E2E tests covering core user flows: game creation, player addition/editing/deletion, in-game navigation, score adjustments, stopwatch functionality, bulk actions, end game flow

nodes.label: E2E Boundary Testing
nodes.rationale: Boundary and corner case E2E tests for input validation, player limits, negative scores, stopwatch states, bulk selection behavior, accessibility features, and state persistence
